meta.title: Surco — Get your DJ tracks ready in seconds
meta.description: Surco automates DJ track prep: convert to lossless AIFF, WAV or FLAC (or a lighter MP3), tag from Discogs and send to Apple Music in seconds — and catch re-encoded MP3s disguised as lossless.
meta.ogTitle: Surco — Get your DJ tracks ready in seconds
meta.ogDescription: Less prepping, more playing: convert to lossless AIFF, WAV or FLAC (or a lighter MP3), tag from Discogs and organize in seconds. And catch MP3s disguised as lossless.
meta.jsonLdDescription: Audio track organizer for DJs: convert to lossless AIFF, WAV or FLAC, tag from Discogs, send to Apple Music and analyze the spectrum to detect re-encoded MP3s.

betaNote: Surco is in beta — free for now while we shape it. No pricing decided yet.

hero.lede: Drop in your tracks: Surco fills in the artist, title and cover art from the Discogs music database, converts them to full quality and organizes them in your Apple Music library — the hub rekordbox reads from. It even warns you when a file is a low-quality fake, or when you already have the track.
hero.ledeShort: Drop your tracks and Surco fills the metadata from Discogs, converts them to full quality and organizes them in your Apple Music library.

analysis.title: The spectrum doesn't lie
analysis.lede: Some "lossless" files are just low-quality MP3s converted to look hi-fi — and they sound worse on a big system. Surco spots the missing high frequencies and flags the track as suspicious before it ends up in your set.
analysis.suspectCaptionPost: — the highs are gone. A fake.

features.title: The whole flow, in one app

items.title: Import and listen
items.body: Drop your WAV, FLAC, AIFF or MP3 — even whole folders. Surco reads the tags and cover art they already carry, and you can preview any track in the built-in player before converting.

items.title: Tag from Discogs
items.body: Search the release: Surco highlights the right track in the tracklist from the filename and pulls artist, album, year, genre, label, catalog number and cover art in one click, with nothing to set up. Then fill the DJ fields: BPM, key, remixer and grouping.

items.title: Convert to any format
items.body: AIFF, WAV, FLAC or MP3, any to any — lossless or a lighter MP3. Switch format anytime without re-importing, and if it's already in the target format Surco copies it untouched: same bits, instantly. Convert one track or the whole crate in one go.

items.title: Keeps your Traktor cues
items.body: Re-export to the same format (MP3 or AIFF) and Surco copies the audio as-is, keeping your Traktor cue points and beatgrid intact.

items.title: No fakes, no duplicates
items.body: Check the spectrum at a glance and catch low-quality MP3s dressed up as lossless. And as you tag, Surco checks your Apple Music library and warns you if the track is already there.

items.title: Organize it your way
items.body: Artwork and tags are written inside the file, so they show up in Apple Music and rekordbox. Send the track to your Apple Music library, and show only the fields you use, mark what's required and name files with {artist}-{title} templates.

shortcuts.title: Fly without touching the mouse
shortcuts.lede: A command palette and shortcuts for the whole flow. Add, tag, analyze and process track after track without lifting your hands off the keyboard.

speed.title: From minutes to seconds per track
speed.lede: By hand it's a multi-step process —convert, tag, fill in tags, import— jumping between tools. Tedious the moment you repeat it track after track. Surco does it all at the press of a button.

manualSteps.label: Add grouping and tags the tagger leaves out

speed.manualCaption: jumping between tools · repeating on every track
bullets: lossless AIFF, WAV or FLAC, or MP3
speed.footnote: * Manual flow shown as an example; steps and tools vary from person to person. Estimated times.

steps.title: Drop your tracks
steps.body: Drag your WAV, FLAC, AIFF or MP3. Surco reads the tags and cover art on the fly.

steps.title: Pick the release
steps.body: Search Discogs and apply artist, album, year, genre and cover art in one click.

steps.title: Export and you're done
steps.body: Convert to AIFF, WAV, FLAC or MP3, check the spectrum and send the track to Apple Music.

footer.tagline: Convert, tag and organize your DJ tracks — ready to play in seconds.

download.intel: Intel Mac (2020 or earlier)? Download it here
download.unavailable: The download isn't available yet — we're polishing the first version.
download.windows: It's coming to Windows too, with everything the same except the automatic send to Apple Music.
